rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Mandatory Relief From Entry Of Default Against Paul Kraaijvanger; Defts Motionto Set Aside Unilaterally Obtained Sanction Orders And To Quash Related Writ Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 31, 2013, line 2 - DEFENDANT PAUL KRAAIJVANGER's Motion For Mandatory Relief From Entry Of Default Against Paul Kraaijvanger; Defendant Real Equity's Motionto Set Aside Unilaterally Obtained Sanction Orders And To Quash Related Writ Of Execution. Defendant Kraaijvanger's motions are DENIED, while untimely, opposition addressing merits was filed. (1) Kraaijvanger admits service (Kraaijvanger Declaration 16). (2) Uchiyama deemed counsel by admission to Court and counsel (Ex. H to Martin Declaration; Uchiyama Declaration 12); (3) Uchiyama's decision not to timely respond is strategic, not neglect (Uchiyama Declaration 9-10); (4) re ex-parte application-Uchiyama was on notice of contents and extent of Plaintiff's October 25, 2013 Ex-parte request (Martin Declaration Ex. K) to which she did not object (Uchiyama Declaration 12). (5) Uchiyama does not deny receiving Notices of Motion to Compel Compliance with Court's Order. Defendant Real Equity's Motion to Set Aside October 25, 2012 Order and Sanction Orders; and to Quash Writ of Execution issued on April 22, 2013 is GRANTED. Plaintiff provides no evidence that Defendant Real Equity Group was properly served with Summons and Complaint. Jack Martin, non - registered process server, declares under penalty of perjury in Proof of Service dated October 8, 2012 and filed at 4:13 p.m. on October 9, 2012 that he served "Paul Kraaijvanger ¿as agent for service of process for Real Equity Group" on October 9, 2012 at 5:00 p.m. Not credible re dates. Proof of Service filed on May 16, 2013 merely reiterates same service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must notify the other party of its intent to appear by 4 p.m. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. CARL BEASLEY et al

rulings.Case Title: SIULING KONG VS. NLN LIAH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 31, 2013, line 8 - DEFENDANT NASRI SAMARA's Re- Notice MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Samara's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED without prejudice as to counsel neglect. No competent evidence that default resulted from both Heath and Olsen neglect. DENIED with prejudice as to Samara, no excusable neglect, mistake or inadvertence shown. =(501/REQ)
